sourceEvidence.label: Round-06 final batch validations, Track A/B/C audits, threat model, and exact-origin browser evidence

assessment.summary: Six repeated control-owner boundaries support structural hardening proposals; none is implemented, and central adoption of the corrected 101/22/65 final counts remains pending.

nonNegotiables: Preserve every AGENTS.md section 2 safety invariant.
Do not treat model text or public-API strings as safety, provenance, or workflow authority.
Do not implement or modify repository source from this design analysis.
Retain human clinical attestation and reviewer ownership.
Treat needs-review candidates as unresolved rather than promoted or dismissed.
assumptions: The public login-free Spring/React architecture and OpenAI-compatible provider remain.
Fixture mode, Redis, MariaDB, and government/Naver integrations remain required.
No measured latency, memory, cache-hit, or telemetry-volume budget was supplied.
Clinical, product/accessibility, privacy, operations, and release/security review gates are available before implementation.
The moving workspace is not evidence for immutable-target behavior.

evidenceRegistry.title: Exact-origin Naver console and network capture

opportunities.title: Create a typed conversation release boundary
opportunities.summary: Compile hostile model candidates into a server-owned released answer bound to exact evidence and reviewed semantic policy.
opportunities.diagnosis: Role normalization, external-context authority, grounding, semantic checks, server-owned fields, and rendering are distributed conventions rather than one complete release decision.

evidence.claim: Schema-valid diagnostic, curative, and dangerous word-form dosing prose reached the final HTTP answer.
Client-authored assistant history is preserved while safety prescans consume only user text.
Government narrative is elevated into privileged model context.
A single explicit authority transition would reduce recurrence across roles, context, identity, semantics, and rendering.

desiredInvariants: All client history and external strings remain untrusted data.
Only ReleasedAnswer can cross the backend-to-browser medical-answer boundary.
Every released medical/provenance field has an exact evidence binding or server-authored policy decision.
Semantic policy is clinically reviewed and reason-coded without logging rejected text.

options.title: Consolidated local guards
options.summary: Keep the answer DTO while strengthening role filtering, context delimiting, exact identity joins, and semantic checks.

findingCoverage.rationale: Adds explicit prose checks to the existing release path.
Delimits external context but retains the shared prompt boundary.
Normalizes the known client role path.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known role, context, identity, and prose gaps gain explicit checks, while future field drift remains.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Replay sealed provider cases and mutate every answer field/role.

tradeoffs.assessment: Checks are bounded in-process work beneath existing response limits.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Benchmark maximum-size answers against the current validator.

tradeoffs.assessment: No new retained representation is required.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare allocation profiles for maximum-size answers.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known unsafe candidates fail closed, but whole-answer rejection can reduce availability.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure legitimate-answer rejection and degradation outcomes.

tradeoffs.assessment: Reason codes improve diagnosis if they remain value-free.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify stage/reason correlation without user content.

tradeoffs.assessment: The public wire DTO can remain unchanged.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run current FE-BE contract coverage.

residualRisks: Distributed checks can drift when fields or branches are added.
Unreviewed semantic filters can over-reject legitimate answers.

options.title: Candidate-to-release compiler
options.summary: Make CandidateAnswer unrenderable until a complete server-owned compiler emits ReleasedAnswer.

findingCoverage.rationale: All answer prose requires a semantic release decision.
Translations bind to exact evidence fields before release.
External data enters a data-only evidence projection.
Conversation normalization removes caller-selected authority.

tradeoffs.assessment: One complete type transition owns role, identity, semantic, and server-field authority.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Prove no controller/UI path accepts CandidateAnswer.

tradeoffs.assessment: A bounded validation/copy step is added; upstream I/O should still dominate.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Benchmark CPU and tail latency at maximum response size.

tradeoffs.assessment: Candidate and released objects coexist briefly.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare peak allocations per chat request.

tradeoffs.assessment: Typed per-field degradation can avoid unnecessary whole-answer loss; the compiler becomes critical path.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject each field and assert deterministic released/degraded/refused outcomes.

tradeoffs.assessment: Complete release reason codes support value-free audit trails.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Reconstruct release decisions by request ID and reason only.

tradeoffs.assessment: Internal and possibly FE-BE answer contracts must change together.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify an adapter/compatibility boundary and rollback path.

residualRisks: Clinical policy can still be wrong.
Provider retention and malicious but correctly identified upstream facts remain outside this boundary.

opportunities.recommendation: Use the explicit release compiler after clinical/product review; retain local guards until it is authoritative.

opportunities.title: Own emergency and allergy transitions in one server state machine
opportunities.summary: Normalize raw safety events once and make forbidden model/UI transitions and output combinations unrepresentable.
opportunities.diagnosis: Emergency/allergy truth is split among regexes, retrieval shortcuts, model fields, answer IDs, frontend state, and picker refreshes.

evidence.claim: Emergency urgency and a medicine card survived together in a sealed final response.
Common reaction language bypasses the fail-closed allergy path.
Model answerId can select a server-only allergy workflow.
Central transition ownership can handle fail-open and over-strong fail-closed behavior together.

desiredInvariants: Raw text and exclusions are normalized before any model call.
Only server events transition safety state.
Emergency and unresolved-allergy states structurally cannot contain model medicine suggestions.
Clinical rules and copy are human-reviewed and versioned.

options.title: Expanded deterministic guards
options.summary: Add missing categories, negation/reaction handling, exclusion preservation, and state/action/card checks in place.

findingCoverage.rationale: Rejects emergency-plus-card combinations.
Adds common reaction declarations.
Adds acknowledged emergency categories.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known input/output gaps close, while split ownership remains.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Mutation-test categories, declarations, actions, and card exclusions.

tradeoffs.assessment: Bounded local classification/checks add little work.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Benchmark maximum conversation text.

tradeoffs.assessment: No retained state structure is required.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare allocation per classification.

tradeoffs.assessment: Fewer misses but possible false-positive lock-in from expanded rules.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Clinician-review affirmative, negated, historical, and ambiguous corpus outcomes.

tradeoffs.assessment: Rule-specific reason codes can expose drift.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify versioned rule/reason telemetry without text.

tradeoffs.assessment: Existing DTOs and flow remain.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run current API/browser contract tests.

residualRisks: Backend and frontend representations can diverge.
Clinical false positives/negatives remain distributed.

options.title: Owned safety state machine
options.summary: Use reviewed events/states and a server composer; the model explains only in states that permit it.

findingCoverage.rationale: Emergency output type cannot contain cards.
Model output has no transition capability.
Lossless unresolved exclusion state prevents silent no-match.
Model urgency is not authoritative state.

tradeoffs.assessment: One reviewed transition table owns all model/UI permissions and output combinations.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exhaustively test every state/event transition and serialized output.

tradeoffs.assessment: Deterministic normalization/state transition is bounded.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure classification latency on maximum input.

tradeoffs.assessment: Only small event/state objects are added.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare per-request allocation.

tradeoffs.assessment: Explicit ambiguity states reduce silent fallthrough; the machine is a critical choke point.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject normalization and assert server-authored safe fallback.

tradeoffs.assessment: Rule version and transition reasons are auditable.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Reconstruct safety decisions without raw text.

tradeoffs.assessment: FE-BE state/action contracts and clinical governance must migrate together.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify comparison adapter and state-exhaustive browser rendering.

residualRisks: Human-approved rules can still be incomplete.
Unknown clinical cross-reactivity remains outside software authority.

opportunities.recommendation: Select the owned state machine only after clinical and product/accessibility approval; keep direct guards during migration.

opportunities.title: Carry identity, freshness, and data mode in a provenanced evidence envelope
opportunities.summary: Make query satisfaction and provenance inseparable from cached, fallback, grounded, and rendered facts.
opportunities.diagnosis: Adapters and caches return ordinary values, so downstream services reconstruct identity, time, and mode from the current request.

evidence.claim: Cached pharmacy facts are restamped as freshly retrieved.
A fixed fixture product can be bound to an arbitrary requested ID.
Cached drug facts lose original observation time.
Treating provenance as part of the value reduces cross-adapter/cache recurrence.

desiredInvariants: Every fact records stable provider identity and the query contract it satisfied.
Agency, observation time, data mode, and fallback chain survive caching unchanged.
Fixture evidence cannot serialize as live and unrelated fallback cannot satisfy a request.
Grounding/source references join on stable identity rather than display text.

options.title: Binding checks and cache namespaces
options.summary: Patch endpoint identity checks, mode/origin keys, timestamp preservation, and fallback labeling.

findingCoverage.rationale: Preserves cached observation time.
Binds returned ID to requested ID.
Binds warnings to requested medicine.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known query/mode/time mismatches gain direct checks; future paths can omit them.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Replay every fixed-fixture and restamping case.

tradeoffs.assessment: Correct key partitioning lowers unsafe cache reuse.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare hit rate and latency by mode/origin/query.

tradeoffs.assessment: More cache key variants can coexist.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure Redis key count and bytes under representative workloads.

tradeoffs.assessment: Wrong results fall to unavailable/miss, improving integrity but reducing fallback availability.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exercise live/fixture/hybrid outage paths.

tradeoffs.assessment: Per-adapter binding/mode reason codes aid diagnosis.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify cache/fallback reasons in value-free telemetry.

tradeoffs.assessment: Affected caches require versioning/invalidation.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Test old-entry treatment as miss, never fresh evidence.

residualRisks: Identity/freshness logic remains repeated.
New caches/adapters can omit the required tuple.

options.title: Provenanced evidence envelope
options.summary: Carry query identity, provider ID, agency, observation time, mode, and fallback chain in one typed value.

findingCoverage.rationale: Observation time is immutable envelope data.
Fixture/hybrid mode is type-carried and cannot become live.
The envelope must satisfy the exact query contract.
Drug caches preserve original evidence metadata.

tradeoffs.assessment: Identity/freshness/mode cannot be detached from the value silently.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Property-test all query/mode/origin mutations.

tradeoffs.assessment: More metadata and correct partitioning add serialization and misses.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure endpoint latency and cache hit rate by workload.

tradeoffs.assessment: Each cached value carries metadata and namespaces no longer collide.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure serialized bytes and peak Redis memory.

tradeoffs.assessment: Invalid evidence becomes explicit unavailable/unknown; broad migration can introduce adapter errors.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject old cache, wrong identity, stale time, and mode mismatch.

tradeoffs.assessment: Uniform evidence reasons expose fallback/freshness behavior.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Audit sourceRef and telemetry from the same envelope identity.

tradeoffs.assessment: Drug/facility/cache/sourceRef representations must migrate coherently.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Validate versioned serializers and old-entry invalidation.

residualRisks: Authoritative upstream facts can still be wrong.
Freshness policy and provider identity mappings require human ownership.

opportunities.recommendation: Adopt the envelope as the cross-domain destination while preserving direct adapter binding checks.

opportunities.title: Replace value-bearing logs with a correlated event contract
opportunities.summary: Record request/build/mode/stage/outcome facts without health, location, profile-capability, provider-body, or secret values.
opportunities.diagnosis: Current logging preserves sensitive values yet omits the correlation and runtime identity required for reliable root-cause attribution.

evidence.claim: Accepted health-related search terms persist in logs.
Malformed chat text can reach catch-all full-Throwable logging.
Returned request IDs could not be joined to runtime stacks without timestamp inference.
Value-free stage/build/mode events would improve both privacy and stale-runtime attribution.

desiredInvariants: Every stage event carries request ID, build ID, and data mode.
No telemetry field accepts raw user, location, profile capability, provider body, URL query, or secret values.
Failures are classified before logging and client errors remain generic.
Telemetry export cannot block care requests and has reviewed retention/access.

options.title: Redaction plus MDC repair
options.summary: Remove known values, attach request ID to log patterns, and sanitize exception messages.

findingCoverage.rationale: Removes device capability from known logs.
Removes exact health-related terms.
Sanitizes malformed-body exception logging.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known sensitive sinks close; future free-form strings can recur.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Canary-test every known sensitive value on success/error paths.

tradeoffs.assessment: Redaction/MDC are small bounded log-path work.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare request throughput with representative logging.

tradeoffs.assessment: No new buffers/services are introduced.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare allocation under error bursts.

tradeoffs.assessment: Correlation improves; excessive sanitization can hide failure class detail.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Re-run Track A/B attribution using request IDs only.

tradeoffs.assessment: Direct request-stack joins replace timestamp inference.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify unique request trail across stages.

tradeoffs.assessment: Existing logging stack remains.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run log-format and error-envelope compatibility checks.

residualRisks: New free-form log statements can reintroduce values.
Third-party library logs remain outside local redaction.

options.title: Value-free event contract
options.summary: Use approved typed fields for stage/outcome/count/duration/build/mode and a sanitized exception classifier.

findingCoverage.rationale: The event API has no device-ID field.
The event API records facility operation/reason, not coordinates.
Only term counts/reason classes are representable.
Exceptions are classified before emitting bounded fields.

tradeoffs.assessment: Positive schema prevents arbitrary application value fields.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Mutation-test attempts to add arbitrary strings/values.

tradeoffs.assessment: Structured encoding/export adds bounded work.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Load-test event throughput and request tail latency.

tradeoffs.assessment: Exporter buffers may retain events depending on implementation.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure buffer size/peak RSS during sink outage.

tradeoffs.assessment: Stage trails improve diagnosis; exporter must fail open for request availability.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject backpressure and sink failure.

tradeoffs.assessment: Stable event names/reasons support exact cross-stage analysis.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Reconstruct each Track A/B path from structured events.

tradeoffs.assessment: Call sites and operational queries must migrate.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Inventory all application logs and validate safe bridge coverage.

residualRisks: Ingress/access/vendor logs can still retain data.
Low-cardinality metadata and event retention require privacy review.

opportunities.recommendation: Adopt the positive event schema after immediate value-log removal and privacy/operations approval.

opportunities.title: Model facility availability with an exhaustive result algebra
opportunities.summary: Separate loading, complete, partial, verified-empty, unavailable, unsupported, and tri-state-hours outcomes across backend and UI.
opportunities.diagnosis: Lists, exceptions, booleans, nulls, and UI timing conventions cannot carry search completeness and row certainty consistently.

evidence.claim: Pending lookup renders as completed no-results.
Malformed hospital hours collapse to closed rather than unknown.
One hospital detail failure discards otherwise valid nearby rows.
Map SDK was healthy; facility 500s came from stale runtime, clean controls returned rows, ER was unsupported, and assistant UI had transient false-empty.

desiredInvariants: Verified empty is constructible only after successful completed search.
Failure cannot serialize as empty and unsupported cannot serialize as no coverage.
Hours remain open, closed, or unknown; unknown rows are not discarded by open-now.
Partial provider failure preserves valid facts and one orchestrator owns deadline/quota/cancellation budget.
Frontend rendering is exhaustive over every result state.

options.title: Harden existing branches
options.summary: Add loading, tri-state parsing, per-row degradation, envelope validation, and local fan-out bounds.

findingCoverage.rationale: Adds explicit loading before no-results.
Maps malformed hours to unknown.
Degrades failed details per row.
Rejects malformed success envelopes.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known empty/closed/partial collapse paths are corrected; cross-surface convention remains.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exercise every settled/pending/failure/unknown case.

tradeoffs.assessment: Bounded fan-out can reduce tail work; fewer rows may be enriched.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure call count, time-to-first-row, and completion latency.

tradeoffs.assessment: No persistent new representation is required.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare peak rows/allocations.

tradeoffs.assessment: Per-row degradation preserves valid care locations.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject one/all detail failures and malformed envelopes.

tradeoffs.assessment: Endpoint-specific failure/partial reasons become visible.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify value-free facility outcome telemetry.

tradeoffs.assessment: Most public DTOs can remain; partial warnings may need extension.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run API/browser compatibility coverage.

residualRisks: List completeness remains implicit.
Main map, chat surface, and new adapters can diverge.

options.title: Typed facility result algebra
options.summary: Return complete/partial/verified-empty/unavailable/unsupported outcomes with tri-state rows and one request budget.

findingCoverage.rationale: Loading and verified-empty are distinct types.
Tri-state hours survive serialization/rendering.
Partial outcome retains valid rows.
Unknown-hours rows are a valid result, not filter failure.
Malformed provider response cannot construct VerifiedEmpty.

tradeoffs.assessment: Outcome collapse becomes harder across backend and UI.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Prove exhaustive serialization/rendering of every algebra case.

tradeoffs.assessment: Orchestrator budgets bound fan-out; metadata cost is small.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Benchmark call budget, time-to-first-row, and tail latency.

tradeoffs.assessment: Outcome/degradation metadata adds bounded bytes.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare response size and peak allocations.

tradeoffs.assessment: Partial/unavailable states preserve truth; orchestrator is a new critical path.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject adapters, deadlines, cancellation, and quota exhaustion.

tradeoffs.assessment: Uniform outcomes and budget reasons support alerting/capacity review.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify outcome and upstream-call telemetry.

tradeoffs.assessment: API and every facility UI consumer must migrate exhaustively.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Contract-test old/new adapters and forbid partial/unavailable-to-empty mapping.

residualRisks: Budgets/caps can still omit a real facility.
Copy can still miscommunicate partial or unknown results without human review.

opportunities.recommendation: Use the result algebra after direct P0/P1 fixes, aligning row provenance with the evidence-envelope proposal.

opportunities.title: Run the demo from an attested artifact under one owning supervisor
opportunities.summary: Bind source, dependencies, classes, resources, configuration, data mode, process, and port identity before readiness.
opportunities.diagnosis: Lexical/path/name guards and manual process startup cannot prove which coherent artifact a fixed demo port is serving.

evidence.claim: No-verify commits can bypass the secret pre-commit control.
Nested .env paths bypass filename protection.
A stale JVM from a deleted worktree produced missing-class/resource chat failures.
The same stale runtime returned facility 500s while clean servers returned rows.
Publication and execution should share one artifact-identity boundary instead of heuristic/process-memory controls.

desiredInvariants: Every demo runtime exposes immutable source/tree/build/resource/config/data-mode identity.
Only a launcher/supervisor that verified the manifest owns the demo port and child process.
Backend/frontend compatibility and required resources are checked before readiness.
CI content scanning and a browser-public environment allowlist are authoritative publication controls.
Rollback selects another coherent verified bundle, never an unknown surviving process.

options.title: Repaired guards plus launch preflight
options.summary: Fix guard gaps, require clean builds, reject stale ports/classpaths/resources, and expose build/mode identity.

findingCoverage.rationale: Repairs known command parser bypasses.
Expands protected secret paths.
Makes hook installation failure visible.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known secret/workflow/stale-launch paths close when prescribed tools are used.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Replay bypasses and stale-port/classpath/resource startup.

tradeoffs.assessment: Checks affect startup, not request path.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure preflight/startup duration.

tradeoffs.assessment: No resident component is added.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Confirm runtime RSS unchanged.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known incoherent runtime is rejected; manual bypass remains possible.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Start via prescribed and manual paths and verify detection.

tradeoffs.assessment: Build/mode metadata and preflight failures aid demo diagnosis.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify browser/health identity and actionable failures.

tradeoffs.assessment: Existing build outputs and local workflow remain.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exercise macOS/Windows documented launch paths.

residualRisks: Manual process startup can bypass preflight.
Heuristic secret scanners and local hooks are not publication attestation.

options.title: Attested bundle and owning supervisor
options.summary: Build a manifest-bound immutable bundle and let one supervisor verify it, own the port, and expose readiness identity.

findingCoverage.rationale: Authoritative publication gate limits impact even if a local hook is bypassed.
CI scans content of the complete bundle/repository input.
Reviewed content policy replaces broad placeholder suppression as authority.
Local installation is defense in depth rather than the sole publication control.

tradeoffs.assessment: Execution/publication bind to coherent verified artifact identity.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Attempt mismatched source/resource/config/port/secret bundles.

tradeoffs.assessment: CI and startup digest verification add time; request path remains neutral.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure bundle build and verification duration.

tradeoffs.assessment: A small supervisor and manifest remain resident.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure supervisor and child RSS.

tradeoffs.assessment: Incoherent artifacts never become ready; supervisor is a new dependency.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fault-inject crash/restart/rollback/port conflicts.

tradeoffs.assessment: Bundle lifecycle, port lease, manifest, and possibly signing keys require ownership.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run incident and rollback exercises.

tradeoffs.assessment: CI/release/demo operating model changes.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Verify current development paths and a documented verified fallback.

residualRisks: Compromised CI/signing authority can attest malicious content.
Dependencies and operator configuration still require independent controls.

opportunities.recommendation: Use the attested bundle for demo/release while repairing local guards as immediate defense in depth.

openQuestions: Which proposal options do clinical, product/accessibility, privacy, operations, and release/security owners approve?
When will the concurrent repository diff clear so the corrected batches can receive fresh central adoption?
What measured latency, memory, cache, quota, telemetry, and startup budgets should govern design selection?
Which needs-review candidates gain or lose relevance after their external or clinical proof gaps are resolved?
